description_long: This object is a double grave marker constructed from polished white and gray marble, featuring two prominent Orthodox-style crosses at the head of each grave. The base is a rectangular slab with subtle veining patterns typical of marble, and the surface is smooth and reflective. Each cross is solid and geometric, rising vertically from the head of the grave, and is crafted from the same marble as the base. The overall design is minimalist, with no visible inscriptions or decorative engravings, emphasizing the clean lines and symmetry of the monument. The structure appears heavy and immovable, suitable for permanent installation in a cemetery or memorial park. The color palette is dominated by shades of white and gray, giving the object a solemn and dignified appearance.
description: A double marble grave marker with two Orthodox-style crosses, featuring a smooth, veined white and gray surface and a minimalist design.
description_view_0: From this top-down angle, the rectangular marble base and the two upright crosses are clearly visible, highlighting the symmetry and veining of the marble.
description_view_1: This head-on view emphasizes the front face of the two Orthodox crosses and the depth of the marble base, showing the monument's height and solid construction.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, displaying the backs of the two crosses and the marble slab, with the same veining and geometric lines.
description_view_3: This rear head-on view again focuses on the crosses' backs and the marble base, with no additional features or inscriptions visible.
